mnemonics: Make Kids Do Right: mkdir() creates, rmdir() removes.
Remember the 'path': Both need a string path to the directory.
Check the 'Mode': mkdir() requires a permission mode argument.
keyPoints: mkdir(const char *pathname, mode_t mode) creates a new, empty directory with the specified pathname and permissions.
rmdir(const char *pathname) deletes an empty directory specified by the pathname.
Both functions return 0 on success and -1 on failure, with errno set to indicate the error.
commonMistakes: Not checking the return value for failure (-1).
Attempting to use rmdir() on a non-empty directory (it will fail).
Forgetting to set the correct file mode (permissions) when using mkdir(), often leading to a directory that the user cannot access.
